observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal SWM Board to follow-up in writing the status of the approval of their MSWMP and update the plan annually to ensure the sustainability and effectiveness their solid waste management.

The Municipality was not able to implement, assess and monitor the projects, programs and activities (PPAs) for the proper Solid Waste Management (SWM) in the LGU not in keeping with the Municipality of Oas Integrated Solid Waste Management Ordinance (ISWMO) of 2017, thus may cause potential risks to the environment and public health.

We recommended that Management:

Expedite the procurement of the garbage disposal facility services to properly dispose the residual wastes in the RCA;

Take the necessary actions to be able to expand their collection of solid wastes in the households and establishments of the Municipality; and

Procure the necessary materials to operationalize their composting facility and establish the Municipal Common Marketing Facility to improve their solid waste management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Property Officer to facilitate the immediate disposal of the aforementioned unserviceable properties and waste materials to recoup any salvage value thereof.  Additionally, we recommend the following measures:
Create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee, whose members are required to attend relevant trainings on property management, particularly on the disposal of unserviceable properties and waste materials, should they lack the necessary technical competence;
Conduct an appraisal of the unserviceable properties and waste materials and furnish the auditor a copy of the appraisal report thereof; and
Dispose the unserviceable properties based on the most appropriate mode of disposal pursuant to the provisions of Sections 379 to 381 of RA No. 7160 and COA Circular Nos. 89-296 and 92-386 following the procedures shown in flow chart of the disposal of property (Annex B).
Further, for the properties damaged during typhoon Rolly, we recommended that the Management:
direct the concerned AO’s to immediately submit a request for relief from accountability and complete supporting documents enumerated in COA Audit Memorandum No. 92-751 (Annex C) for all the properties damaged;
direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the loss in the notes to financial statements pending the result of the request for relief from accountability and when request is granted, prepare a JEV to derecognize the assets in the books of accounts. If request is denied, the loss shall be taken up as a receivable from the accountable officer/persons liable and shall be credited to the appropriate asset account.

We recommended that the Management:
direct the concerned AO’s to immediately submit a request for relief from accountability and complete supporting documents enumerated in COA Memorandum No. 92-751 (Annex C) for all the properties damaged and lost in the aforementioned fire incident; and
direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the loss in the notes to financial statements pending the result of the request for relief from accountability and when request is granted, prepare a JEV to derecognize the assets in the books of accounts. If request is denied, the loss shall be taken up as a receivable from the accountable officer/persons liable and shall be credited to the appropriate asset account.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
properly record the collections and deposits to reflect the correct balances of Cash in Local Treasury and Cash in Bank account in the financial statements; and
have a thorough analysis of the total reconciling item amounting to ₱160,051.47 and to prepare JEV to take up reconciling items already determined as book error.

We recommended that Management:
Provide the Audit Team copies of the MOA for all unliquidated fund transfers as of December 31, 2021. Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant and the Head of the Implementing Offices (IOs) to ensure that all fund transfers from NGAs and other sources are properly supported with the required documents as provided in COA Circular No. 94-003 and COA Circular No. 2012-002;
Require the IOs to properly coordinate with the Municipal Accounting Office and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to ensure that all fund transfers are properly credited to the trust fund account and properly recorded in the books of the Agency; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to make the following correcting entry:
Due to NGAs                                 ₱12,916,432.14
Trust Liabilities-DRRM          		₱12,916,432.14
To transfer to Trust Liabilities-DRRM account funds received from
the National Government intended as DRRM fund.
Henceforth, she shall record under the Trust Liabilities-DRRM Fund account donations and financial assistance received from the National Government and from other sources.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to require the disbursing officer to complete the details in the report of disbursement such as date of the liquidation of the related cash advance.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to indicate the date of all the transactions in the SL of the disbursing officer as well as references such as the disbursement voucher and check numbers in the liquidation of cash advances. Additionally, instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure complete supporting documents in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 before the issuance of cash advance to the disbursing officer.
Designate additional disbursing officer who will be granted with other cash advances and be responsible for liquidating the same.

We recommended that Management instruct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer to:
Require all collecting officers to prepare RCDs at the close of each business day and effect remittance of their collections daily to the Liquidating Officer. In the case of the deputized collectors in the barangays, require their remittance at least once a month or as soon as the collections reaches the threshold of ₱5,000.00.
Require the Liquidating Officer to verify the Report of Collections and Deposits submitted by the collectors and prepare the RCDs of the said remittances and deposit the same to the depository bank daily or the next banking day.
Require the Liquidating Officer to check the Statement of Accountable Forms as to initial balances on hand, receipts, issues and the ending balances on hand, make physical count of accountable forms remaining in the custody of collectors and check the same against new balances on hand column. Henceforth, accountable forms shall be issued only to those accountable officers who have religiously and completely remitted their collections in compliance with the provisions of Section 30 of the Manual on NGAs Volume I.
Require all the Accountable Officers with unaccounted accountable forms to formally render a report thereon. In the event that these can no longer be located/presented, a request for relief from accountability for the loss of accountable forms shall be made in accordance with the requirements under COA Circular No. 84-233 dated August 2, 1984.
Further, we recommended that Management take appropriate action against the accountable officers for failure to account for missing accountable forms under their custody despite various reminders and follow-ups.

We recommended that Management ensure the implementation of these projects in the ensuing years to attain its commitment to its constituents to deliver the benefits that can be derived from the projects.
We recommended that the Municipal Government optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF in order to strengthen its disaster preparedness and avoid potential damages during disasters.

We recommended that Management identify in its annual Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) the specific programs/projects/activities that are to be charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years under a separate caption in the plan to promote better fund management and fiscal accountability.

Further, we recommended that the GFPS/TWG continuously ensure that the required budget of at least 5% of the total Annual Budget is appropriated for GAD related Programs, Activities and Projects and to continue to design and implement PAPs that recognize fundamental equality before law of women and men and those which contribute to poverty alleviation and economic empowerment, especially of marginalized women; the protection, promotion, and fulfillment of women’s human rights; and the practice of gender-responsive governance.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the GAD Focal Person and the TWG to establish fully the GAD database for them to have an effective basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation. Likewise, GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team shall also be established pursuant to the aforecited JMC for them to monitor and evaluate the Municipality’s GAD policies, programs and projects.

Assign or hire personnel who will take full responsibility as an ESWM focal person of the LGU for him/her to monitor a systematic, comprehensive and ecological solid waste management program required under RA No. 9003.
Update the status of the submitted Ten-Year ESWM Plan of the LGU so that they will have a basis/guide in implementing proper solid waste management.
Coordinate with the barangays the immediate rehabilitation of the dilapidated MRFs. Also provide assistance in the information dissemination to the residents on the use and purpose of the MRFs established in their barangay and the proper segregation process of the wastes/garbage.
Provide necessary budgetary allocation for the MENRO in order for the ESWM to operate effectively for the benefit of the residents of the municipality.
Establish a sanitary land fill in the Municipality in accordance with guidelines set forth in RA 9003.

We recommended that Management submit the following:
Submit a document of authority such as Office Orders or Memoranda, indicating the names of persons who were assigned to COVID 19 treatment/quarantine facilities directly catering to or in contact with COVID-19 patients, PUIs, and PUMs, and the period of their assignment;
DTRs or duly accomplished time records of employees; and
Certified or authorized document by the head of agency/office establishing that frontline HRHs physically reported for work at their assigned work stations during the state of national emergency.

We recommended to Management to submit the following:
Documents of authority for the entitlement of the AHDP allowance such as certification or office/assignment orders covering assignment of HRHs to the City’s COVID 19 treatment/quarantine facilities, and whose official duties and responsibilities are directly related to the healthcare response of the government to COVID-19;
DTRs or duly accomplished time records of employees; and
Certified or authorized document by the head of agency/office establishing that frontline HRHs physically reported for work at their assigned work stations during the state of national emergency.

We recommended that, henceforth, the Management prioritize urgent appropriation ordinances, particularly those intended to address the COVID 19 pandemic to ensure that there is sufficient, adequate and readily available funding for the immediate delivery of basic necessities and services to families and individuals affected by the pandemic and to promote and protect the collective interests of the people of Oas in these challenging times.

We recommended that Management:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the reglementary period for the submission to the Auditor of the Bank Reconciliation Statements, together with the corresponding Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs), paid/cancelled checks and original copies of bank statements and Debit and Credit Memos for review and verification, and

Make representation with the depository bank to ensure prompt issuance of the bank statements to the LGU. Otherwise, secure copies of snapshots of the bank statements to facilitate the preparation of the BRS.

We recommended that Management assign an Officer-in-Charge at the Office of the Municipal Assessor until a Municipal Assessor is appointed so as not to disrupt the operations and services of the Office.

Thereafter, the Office of the Municipal Assessor shall furnish the Municipal Treasurer the assessment roll containing the list of all persons whose real properties have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties.

Further, the Municipal Accountant to record the RPT/SET Receivables for CY 2021 based on the initial data provided by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer, until such time that the correct/updated data can be provided by the Office of the Municipal Assessor. An adjusting entry shall then be made upon receipt of the correct amount of tax receivable based on the updated data.

We recommended that Management form a Reconciliation Team composed of the designated Property Officer and at least one capable member coming from the Municipal Accounting Office and the Municipal Engineering Office (for the infrastructure assets, buildings and other structures) who shall be temporarily relieved of their regular duties until such time that the reconciliation process is completed. 

Further, take appropriate immediate remedial measures, such as allowing the Reconciliation Team to render overtime services and the hiring of additional manpower thru job order contract to assist the team, subject to the availability of funds, in order to facilitate the reconciliation process.

We recommended that the Management immediately hire a staff, thru job order contract, pending the approval of the HRMPSB by the Sanggunian, subject to availability of funds, who shall assist the designated Property Officer to maintain property subsidiary records such as Property Cards and Local Road Network Property Cards (LRNPCs) of all existing constructed and acquired properties of the Municipality based on the documents and records such as Certificate of Completion and Acceptance, As-Built Plans, etc., of the MEO. 

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Engineer provide the designated Property Officer with the relevant information, documents and records such as Certificate of Completion and Acceptance, As-Built Plans, etc. of all the existing constructed and acquired properties of the Municipality. Thereafter, immediately or on regular basis inform the designated Property Officer of all the completed projects after their completion and final acceptance and provide to him copies of relevant documents and records thereof.

Moreover, the Inventory Committee to determine and indicate in the RPCPPE the physical condition of the infrastructure assets, buildings and other structures for proper measurement of its carrying amount in the financial statements and to update the current use of lots/land for proper classification in the books by the Municipal Accountant; and

Finally, for the Sanggunian to expedite their review and approval of the HRMPSB submitted by the Management to facilitate the organization and establishment of the General Services Office to be headed by a permanent Department Head to take on the duties and responsibilities that are provided under Section 490 of the Local Government Code, due to the increasing number of assets owned by the LGU that needs to be accounted for.

We commended Management, particularly the Municipal Accounting Office and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, for deducting the mandatory employees’ premium contributions, including the employer’s share as well as all taxes due and causing the prompt remittance thereof to the concerned agencies.

Municipal Accountant and Property Custodian to maintain PPELCs and PCs, in accordance with the forms prescribed in Annexes 9 and 37 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II, respectively and conduct periodic reconciliation of the accounting and property records; and

require the BAC and the Municipal Accountant  to submit a written explanation for the non-compliance of Sections 20 and 21.2.1.(a) of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and for the failure to require the supporting documents during the pre-audit, relative to that procurement of  infrastructure project activity/ transaction, respectively and furnish a copy to the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management:
Designate specific personnel to work on the transfer of the titles of the three lots in the name of the Municipality and appropriate funds to cover the cost of such transfers; and
Require the Municipal Accounting Office to maintain a Real Property Ledger Card for Land.

We recommended that Management require the:
Inventory Committee/Team to conduct a thorough and complete physical inventory of the Municipality’s properties, thereafter, prepare the RPCPPE in the prescribed form as shown in Annex 49 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II, and submit the same to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year;
Municipal Accountant and Property Custodian to maintain PPELCs and PCs, in accordance with the forms prescribed in Annexes 9 and 37 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II, respectively and conduct periodic reconciliation of the accounting and property records; and
Municipal Accountant to prepare the Depreciation Schedule in such a way that complete information of the PPE are described therein clearly and concisely to facilitate the review of the recorded depreciation thereof and cross-checking with the RPCPPE.
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the PPE items in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that:
The Property Custodian keep a complete and updated LRNPCs to serve as a means of checking the accuracy of the Road Network account maintained by the Municipal Accountant.
Both the Property Custodian and the Municipal Accountant conduct regular reconciliation of the balances of their records, at least on a quarterly basis, to ensure that their records are consistent with each other.
The Municipal Engineer be required to furnish the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian complete description and cost segregation of road components for completed road projects as basis for recording in their respective subsidiary records.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Engineering Office strictly monitor the on-going projects to ensure that these are completed according to specifications and within the timeframes stipulated in the contracts thereof.

We recommended that Management allocate/appropriate at least one per cent of its DRRM funds for MDM in compliance with Section 4.19 of NDRRMC Memo Circular No. 19 s. 2016 and, thereafter, integrate in their LDRRM Plan policies, standards, guidelines, systems and procedures for MDM operations and research and development.

We recommended that the MDRRMO and Municipal Accountant require the recipient Punong Barangays to submit the pertinent RDLs and forward the same to the Auditor.  Otherwise, the non-submission of these documents shall warrant the issuance of a Notice of Suspension or Disallowance.
Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and Section 168(c) of GAAM, Volume I, on complete documentation to protect the interest of the government and to prevent unwarranted disbursements of public funds.

We recommended that Management prioritize the creation of the HRMPSB through a resolution by the Sanggunian to facilitate the recruitment and promotion of personnel in order to fill up vacant positions to enhance its operations and services.

We recommended that Management direct the GAD-FPS to establish a GAD Database that will contain statistics and sex-disaggregated data systematically gathered and regularly updated to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation and subjected to gender analysis, for proper identification of existing gender issues to ensure that the approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GPB are able to address these issues.

We recommended the Management direct the:
Municipal Accountant to remit in full the unremitted balances of GSIS premiums and loan amortizations that were not remitted in January 2020;
AAO to strictly ensure that the net take home pay of the loan applicant/member after deducting the loan amortization will not fall below the minimum take-home pay of ₱5,000.00; and
Municipal Accountant to make sure that payments to GSIS shall take priority over and above the payment of any and all obligations as stated in RA No. 8291 (GSIS Act).

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that:

The General Services Officer/Property Custodian initiate the conduct of physical inventory taking of all Supplies of the Municipality at year-end and prepare and submit the corresponding Reports of Physical Count of Inventory (RPCI) to the Audit Team to establish the existence, accuracy and validity of reported balances in the financial statements; and

The Municipal Accountant and General Service Officer/Property Custodian maintain a complete and updated Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards and regularly conduct reconciliation thereof so that any discrepancies noted could be promptly adjusted and reconciled.

Use the information in the BRMR and record of the MTO to analyze and identify the business establishments that were not able to secure the business permit and Mayor’s permit for the current and previous years; 

If JIT is not possible or has not yet been created,  instruct the departments implementing business-related regulations and local units of national agencies to conduct individual/separate inspections to verify information declared by the business in the application for business permit and to identify any unregistered businesses; and

Include the presumptive income levels to be used in the PILAA in their revision of the Revenue Code.

We recommended that the Management:

Require the Municipal Treasurer to use the list to send demand letters to all the delinquent taxpayers and prepare statement of the reason/s for such non-collection or non-payment and submit the same to the Sanggunian on or before December thirty-first (31st) of the year immediately succeeding the year in which the delinquencies were incurred, with a request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection provided in the LGC; and

We recommended that Management consider the use of the ATM facility offered by the AGSB in paying the salaries, wages and other benefits of the officials and employees of the LGU, in order to safeguard the government funds against unnecessary risk of possible loss through theft, misuse or misappropriation and to improve cash management with a view to ensuring an efficient and economical use of government funds.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive:

Require the Officials/Department Heads authorized to receive monthly RATA to comply with the abovementioned provisions of the DBM Local Budget Circular No. 103;

Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit a certified list of Officers who are collecting RATA, indicating the number of days they have rendered service, DTR of Department Heads and minutes of meetings of Sangguniang Bayan Sessions for the CY 2017 RATA payments, otherwise this warrant the issuance of audit suspension or disallowance; and henceforth to attach in the payroll the aforecited supporting documents; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the minutes of meetings of Sangguniang Bayan for CY 2017, to verify the validity of the RATA claims of Sangguniang Bayan Members, otherwise this warrant the issuance of audit suspension or disallowance.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Engineer to integrate the Construction Safety and Health Program in the computation of construction costs of all projects to be implemented by the Municipality pursuant to DOLE Department Order No. 13, to ensure the protection and welfare of the workers at the construction site as well as the general public passing around the construction site.

We recommended that Management adopt strategies to enhance the implementation and monitoring of the projects of the Municipality for the prompt completion of the projects to optimize the utilization of the 20 per cent LDF for the attainment of a desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes and for the benefit of its constituents.

We recommended that Management require:

The contractors of all infrastructure projects of the Municipality to secure a Governor’s Permit on sand, gravel and other quarry resources and to pay the corresponding ten percent (10%) taxes and fees to the Province of Albay for sand, gravel and other quarry resources extracted within the Province of Albay; and

The Municipal Accountant to require the submission of document/s evidencing the payment of the tax to the provincial government by the contractor, including the Governor’s Permit, as additional supporting documents for claims of payment of the projects.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that:
The General Services Officer/Property Custodian initiate the conduct of physical inventory taking of all Supplies of the Municipality at year-end and prepare and submit the corresponding Reports of Physical Count of Inventory (RPCI) to the Audit Team to establish the existence, accuracy and validity of reported balances in the financial statements; and
The Municipal Accountant and General Service Officer/Property Custodian maintain a complete and updated Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards and regularly conduct reconciliation thereof so that any discrepancies noted could be promptly adjusted and reconciled.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management:
Investigate and reconcile the difference between the RPCPPE and accounting records amounting to ₱ 37.87 million and thereafter instruct the Inventory Team to conduct a thorough and complete physical inventory of all the agency’s properties; and
Require the Property Custodian-Designate to maintain Property Cards and Municipal Accountant to update and reconcile the PPELC, in accordance with the forms prescribed in Annexes 9 and 37 of the NGAs Manual for LGUs, Volume II, respectively and conduct periodic reconciliation of accounting and property records.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make a final reconciliation and accounting of these account balances to determine whether these needs to be returned to Source Agencies or reverted back to the General Fund and thereafter facilitate the immediate return of unexpended balances to the concerned Source Agency in compliance with Section 6.7 of COA Circular No. 94-013.

We recommended that Management prioritize, optimally utilize and promptly implement Programs, Projects and Activities funded by the 20 per cent DF in accordance with the timelines so that projects chargeable to the fund could be implemented within the most reasonable time to ensure socio-economic benefits projected to be enjoyed by its constituents within the budget year and ensure continuous economic development in the Municipality which are essential in the attainment of its goals and objectives in improving the quality of life of its constituents.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal SWM Board to follow-up in writing the status of the approval of their MSWMP and update the plan annually to ensure the sustainability and effectiveness their solid waste management.

We recommended that Management:
Expedite the procurement of the garbage disposal facility services to properly dispose the residual wastes in the RCA;
Take the necessary actions to be able to expand their collection of solid wastes in the households and establishments of the Municipality; and
Procure the necessary materials to operationalize their composting facility and establish the Municipal Common Marketing Facility to improve their solid waste management.
We recommended that Management impose the necessary fees and fines and penalties to finance the costs of preparing, adopting, and implementing their solid waste management plan.

We recommended that Management:
Provide the necessary budget  and personnel for MENRO;
Establish IEC Team and prepare IEC campaign materials for distribution that should be crafted in accord to the level of understanding of the stakeholders in order to saturate the target stakeholders in its entirety; and
Collaborate IEC campaigns with the Barangay officials to achieve proper coordination and synchronization and establish focal persons for each barangay who shall be responsible in the arrangements and schedules of IEC campaigns.
We recommended that Management give priority for the revision and updating of the 2008 Revenue Code of the Municipality pursuant to Section 191 of the LGC of 1991.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan and concerned officials revisit the provisions of the Revenue Code of the Municipality to make possible amendments that would redound to the best interest of the LGU and the citizenry without going beyond the provisions and requirements of the LGC of 1991.

We recommended that Management:
Use the information in the BRMR and record of the MTO to analyze and identify the business establishments that were not able to secure the business permit and Mayor’s permit for the current and previous years;
If JIT is not possible or has not yet been created,  instruct the departments implementing business-related regulations and local units of national agencies to conduct individual/separate inspections to verify information declared by the business in the application for business permit and to identify any unregistered businesses; and
Impose the necessary taxes, fees and penalties for business establishments that did not renew their business permit and Mayor’s permit for the current and previous years.

We recommended that Management:
Require the taxpayers to submit their proof of business capitalization (e.g. Articles of Incorporation for corporations or sworn statement for sole proprietorship) for new applications and proof of income (e.g. Audited Financial Statements, Income Tax Return and Value Added Tax Returns) for renewal of business permit as basis for computation of business tax that should retained by the MTO in the taxpayer’s permanent file for current and future reference; and
Include the presumptive income levels to be used in the PILAA in their revision of the Revenue Code.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the Assessment Roll of the remaining 52 barangays will be properly sorted per taxpayer using MS Excel and use their file of RPTAR to identify all the delinquent taxpayers to come up with the complete certified list of all RPT delinquencies;
Require the Municipal Treasurer to use the list to send demand letters to all the delinquent taxpayers and prepare statement of the reason/s for such non-collection or non-payment and submit the same to the Sanggunian on or before December thirty-first (31st) of the year immediately succeeding the year in which the delinquencies were incurred, with a request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection provided in the LGC; and
Consider the procurement of computer software for the recording of collection of RPT as well as other taxes for easier monitoring and report preparation to take advantage of the advancement of technology and to cope up with the growing economy.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant (MA) to record the RPT and SET Receivables based on the duly certified list of the Municipal Treasurer (MT) showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for CY 2018 in consonance with Section 20 of the MNGAS for LGUs.
Also Management require the MT to furnish the MA at the beginning of the year, a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year.

We recommended that Management; a) include in the Job Order Contracts a statement of duties and responsibilities to be performed by the JO worker which will define their specific tasks, duties and functions; and b) require the concerned job order workers to submit their individual accomplishment reports duly verified and signed by their immediate supervisors and attach the same to the payroll, in order to facilitate the evaluation of the hiring of JO workers.

We recommended that Management:
 
Require the concerned Accountable Officer to submit the accounts and financial reports to the Treasurer and thereafter to submit to the Accounting Office all the disbursements vouchers and payrolls, official receipts, including the corresponding reports of each month on or before the  fifth day of the ensuing month or pursuant to the abovementioned pertinent provisions of laws, rules and regulations, as applicable;

Direct the Accountant to strictly comply with the timelines on the recording and submission of accounts to the Auditor, within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month, as required by the abovementioned existing law, rules and regulations and look into the possibility of allowing them to render overtime, subject to availability of funds, in order to submit the accounts and financial reports on the mandated deadlines; and

Initiate the necessary administrative and/or criminal action in case of unjustified failure/refusal to effect compliance with the foregoing requirements by subordinate officials.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive:

Require the Officials/Department Heads authorized to receive monthly RATA to comply with the abovementioned provisions of the DBM Local Budget Circular No. 103;

Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit a certified list of Officers who are collecting RATA, indicating the number of days they have rendered service, DTR of Department Heads and minutes of meetings of Sangguniang Bayan Sessions for the CY 2017 RATA payments, otherwise this warrant the issuance of audit suspension or disallowance; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the minutes of meetings of Sangguniang Bayan for CY 2017, to verify the validity of the RATA claims of Sangguniang Bayan Members, otherwise this warrant the issuance of audit suspension or disallowance.

We recommended that Management require the:

Municipal Engineer to integrate the Construction Safety and Health Program in the computation of construction costs of all projects to be implemented by the Municipality pursuant to DOLE Department Order No. 13, to ensure the protection and welfare of the workers at the construction site as well as the general public passing around the construction sites; and

Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to require interested Contractors to submit DOLE-approved CSHP to ensure the latter’s compliance with Occupational Safety and Health Standards set by the DOLE, as one of the documents that shall form part of the contract.

We recommended that Management require the:
	
Municipal Health Officer to check whether the medicines being requisitioned are included in the latest edition of the PNDF to ensure the medicines are affordable, efficacious and safe for consumption and thereafter issue the certification. If any drug to be requisitioned is not included in the PNDF, a written request for exemption for procurement with corresponding justification addressed to the Head of the National Center for Pharmaceutical Access and Management (NCPAM) should be undertaken; and

Municipal Accountant to ensure that payments for the procurement of medicines are supported with the complete supporting documents to avoid audit suspension/disallowance.

We recommended that the Management strictly adhere and comply with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 96-010 dated August 15, 1996 on the prompt submission of Notices of Deliveries for all procurement of goods including the duly accomplished Inspection and Acceptance Report (IAR).

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

Properly maintain special account in the GF for the 20% EDF, with complete SLs using the Sub-Codes for this Special Account; and

Prepare a post-closing trial balance and financial statements for this Special Account and submit the same to the Audit Team for verification.

We recommended that Management:
	
Require the contractor of installation of streetlights to complete the project as soon as possible and impose applicable sanctions and penalties for the delayed completion of the project by demanding from the contractor the corresponding liquidated damages; and

Adopt strategies to enhance the implementation and monitoring of the projects of the Municipality for the prompt completion of the projects to optimize the utilization of the 20 per cent LDF for the attainment of a desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes and for the benefit of its constituents.

We recommended that Management require the LSB to immediately formulate the required three-year program and to follow the mandated processes in the planning and budgeting for the SEF in accordance with the prescribed timelines as required by DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017, to be able to prepare the CY 2018 SEF Budget and to ensure a more strategic and efficient utilization of the SEF for priority programs and projects pursuant to said Circular.

We recommended that Management, through the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) and the LPDO, facilitate the preparation of the GAD database in order to have an effective basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation and to undertake thorough gender analysis based on relevant data for proper identification of existing gender issues to ensure that the approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GPB are gender responsive.

We recommended that:

LGU Management initiate measures to collect the delinquencies from Market Rentals amounting to ₱0.81 million and employ strategies to intensify collection of delinquent Market Fees to generate additional income for development projects and to improve its collection efficiency; and

The Local Sangguniang Bayan enacts a new Tax Ordinance to adjust the rates to current levels.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to always ensure the completeness of documentation in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and that Management ensure that operating and accounting practices follow established rules, regulations, procedural guidelines, policies, principles and practices that have gained recognition in laws in accordance with COA Circular 2012-003 to establish the validity and propriety of transactions, otherwise lacking supporting documents may result in audit suspensions and disallowances.

Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant shall see to it and ensure that Contractors abide with the implementing rules and regulations of RA No. 9184 pertaining to subject payment, in all procurement activities of the LGU.  

We recommended that Management consider the available equipment of the Municipality and/or have contract with other government Agencies with available equipment necessary in the projects before resorting to the implementation of the projects “by Administration” and to refrain from renting equipment, especially from private contractors, otherwise this warrant the issuance of audit suspension or disallowance.

If there is deviation in the mode of procurement, the BAC, through a Resolution shall justify and recommend the change in the mode of procurement or the updating of the APP to be approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE).

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly enforce the withholding of the ten percent (10%) retention money from the payments made to contractors (up to fifty percent of the value of the works), and ten percent (10%) retention from payments for the goods (except for the exemptions provided in Section 54.5 of the IRR of RA No. 9184) and posting of appropriate securities pursuant to Sections 6.1 and 6.2 of Annex E and Section 62.1 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, otherwise this warrant the issuance of audit suspension wherein the Municipal Accountant shall be one of the persons responsible.

We recommended that Management:

Cause the immediate submission of the afore-cited lacking documents, otherwise, the non-submission of these documents shall warrant the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance;

We recommended that Management require the contractor to complete the project as soon as possible and impose applicable sanctions and penalties for the delayed completion of the project by demanding from the contractor the corresponding liquidated damages, and monitor strictly all contracts of procurement and terminate those that are significantly delayed where liquidated damages may reach more than 10% of the contract price pursuant to Section 68 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

Also, we recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) should properly/completely accomplish the mandatory documents particularly, the Contracts and its supporting documents and the NTP, pursuant to the pertinent provisions of the RIRR of R.A. No. 9184 or corresponding Notices of suspensions/disallowances may be issued relative to this.

We recommended that the personnel in charge of accepting deliveries to notify the Auditor of the time and schedule of deliveries within  twenty four (24) hours from such acceptance pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-010.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort in reviewing/reconciling the Other Assets account to accurately determine the composition of the recorded amount of ₱1,510,975.00. We also recommended the immediate disposal of the unserviceable PPE while it still may command high price to generate additional income to the Municipality. the Property Custodian Officer make a complete inventory of all unserviceable / obsolete properties and prepare a report showing the itemized and complete description of the assets.

the Property Custodian files an application for disposal of unserviceable properties together with the duly accomplished Inventory and Inspection Report for appraisal of the COA Technical Audit Specialist.

the Management thru its Disposal Committee causes the disposal of all unserviceable vehicles/motorcycles in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.
